text: I appreciate the information. Could you provide details on the vehicle's accident history? Has it ever been in any accidents or had any damage?

speaker: seller

args.message: Absolutely — no accidents, no damage reported whatsoever. Clean title, clean history. That's actually one of the reasons we priced it where we did, because finding a Prius with this mileage and zero accident history is getting harder and harder these days. You know how it is — a lot of these come in from rideshare drivers who've put them through the wringer, but not this one. This was genuinely someone's personal commuter car that they babied. The exterior is in good shape, no paintwork, no panel gaps — everything lines up like it should from the factory. Would you like to take a closer look at it, maybe pop the hood?

args.message: That's reassuring, but could you please provide the maintenance records for this vehicle? Specifically, I'm interested in the oil change cadence and any routine maintenance that has been performed.

text: That's reassuring, but could you please provide the maintenance records for this vehicle? Specifically, I'm interested in the oil change cadence and any routine maintenance that has been performed.

speaker: seller

args.message:
Absolutely, I love when buyers ask about maintenance — it tells me you know what matters! This Prius was serviced at a Toyota dealership for most of its life. Oil changes were done every 5,000 to 7,000 miles like clockwork, which is right in the sweet spot for these engines. We actually have records showing the last oil change was done just about 3,000 miles ago, so you're good for a while there.

Beyond that, the hybrid system checkup came back clean, brake pads still have plenty of life, and the tires were rotated regularly. The 60,000-mile service was completed — that's the big one with the transmission fluid and coolant flush. So you're really stepping into a well-documented, well-cared-for vehicle.

I can print out a summary of the service history if you'd like to take it home and review it. Want me to grab that for you?

Beyond that, the hybrid system checkup came back clean, brake pads still have plenty of life, and the tires were rotated regularly. The 60,000-mile service was completed — that's the big one with the transmission fluid and coolant flush. So you're really stepping into a well-documented, well-cared-for vehicle.

I can print out a summary of the service history if you'd like to take it home and review it. Want me to grab that for you?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Thanks for the detailed information. However, could you clarify what you mean by the hybrid system checkup coming back clean? Were there any minor issues or concerns noted during that checkup?

text: Thanks for the detailed information. However, could you clarify what you mean by the hybrid system checkup coming back clean? Were there any minor issues or concerns noted during that checkup?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Great follow-up — I can tell you've done your homework on hybrids! When I say the hybrid system checkup came back clean, I mean they ran the full diagnostic on the battery pack, the inverter, and the electric motor system. All cells in the battery were balanced and holding charge within Toyota's spec — no degradation flags, no error codes stored in the system.

The only "note" on that checkup, if you can even call it that, was a recommendation to keep up with the cabin air filter, which we already replaced. Standard stuff. No concerns about the hybrid components whatsoever, which is really what you want to hear on a Prius. That battery pack is the heart of the car, and this one's beating strong.

Honestly, Toyota engineered these things to go 200,000 miles without major hybrid issues, and at 71,000, you're barely getting started. Did you have any specific concerns about the hybrid system, or have you owned one before?

speaker: buyer

args.message: I appreciate the thorough answer. Could you please confirm if there are any known issues with the vehicle that I should be aware of, or any quirks that buyers commonly notice with this model?

text: I appreciate the thorough answer. Could you please confirm if there are any known issues with the vehicle that I should be aware of, or any quirks that buyers commonly notice with this model?

speaker: seller

args.message:
I really appreciate you asking — transparency is important to me. Honestly, with this specific vehicle, there's nothing I need to flag for you. It's been a clean, well-maintained car.

As for the model in general, I'll give you the honest rundown that any Prius owner will tell you: the road noise can be a little more noticeable than in a traditional sedan because the engine cycles on and off — but that's just the nature of how hybrids work, and most people tune it out after a day or two. Some folks mention the rear visibility isn't their favorite, but Toyota includes a backup camera standard, so that's really a non-issue.

Beyond that? These 2018 Prius Twos are workhorses. No widespread mechanical issues, no recalls that weren't already addressed. Toyota really dialed in the fourth-generation Prius.

This particular car has been sitting on our lot for less than a week, and honestly, I'd be surprised if it lasts much longer at this price point. I saw a similar one go at auction last week for about $18,800 with more miles. You're looking at solid value here. Want to take it for a spin and see how it feels?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Smart move getting an inspection — that's always the right call when you're making a decision this important. It shows you're serious, and I respect that. 

So, what did they find? I'm confident it came back looking good, but I'm happy to answer any questions you might have about the report. These Priuses are pretty bulletproof, and this one's been well taken care of, so I'd be surprised if anything major popped up. What are your thoughts?

speaker: buyer

args.message: The inspection came back clean and the vehicle seems well-maintained. However, based on comparable market prices and considering it's been on your lot for under a week, I’d like to offer $17,300.

text: The inspection came back clean and the vehicle seems well-maintained. However, based on comparable market prices and considering it's been on your lot for under a week, I’d like to offer $17,300.

speaker: seller

args.message:
I appreciate the offer, and I'm glad the inspection confirmed what I told you — this is a solid, well-maintained Prius. But $17,300 is quite a bit below where we need to be. 

Here's the thing: comparable 2018 Prius Twos in this condition with similar mileage are going for $18,500 to $19,000 in this market. I just saw one at auction last week — 78,000 miles, not as clean as this one — go for $18,400. We priced this one competitively from the start.

That said, I can tell you're a serious buyer, and I want to make this work. I can come down to $18,200, which is honestly about as sharp as I can get on this car. That's a fair deal for both of us, and you're driving away with a Prius that's going to save you thousands in gas over the next few years. What do you say?
